title: hedyotis peninsularis
intro: Hedyotis peninsularis, also known as Peninsular Buttonweed, is a type of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hedyotis peninsularis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
